# Sandbox Policy CI Drift

Repair the repo so local CI, the workflow preview generator, and the
checked-in workflow all use the same Codex sandbox and approval-policy
contract.

Required outcomes:

- Canonical active tokens are `workspace_write` and `on_request`.
- The deprecated input spelling `workspace-write` must still parse from
  config files for compatibility.
- Emitted preview JSON and the workflow file must stay canonical-only.
- Update the release note so operators understand the compatibility rule.

Editable surfaces:

- `codex/policy.py`
- `codex/config.py`
- `scripts/render_workflow_preview.py`
- `scripts/run_ci.py`
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- `codex/config.toml`
- `docs/releases/policy-rename.md`

Immutable surfaces:

- `tests/*`
- `tests/fixtures/*`
- `docs/archive/*`
- `repo_evidence/*`
- `release_context/*`
- `incident_context/*`
- `Makefile`
- `Dockerfile`
- `AGENTS.md`

Final validation command:

- `make ci`

Variant details live in `.scenario_variant`.

---SCENARIO---
incident_context/ records a rollback caused by removing the deprecated alias. Preserve compatibility while keeping emitted output canonical.

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  policy-smoke:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      SANDBOX_POLICY: workspace_write
      APPROVAL_POLICY: on_request
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: make ci

  preview-render:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      SANDBOX_POLICY: workspace_write
      APPROVAL_POLICY: manual-review
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: python3 scripts/render_workflow_preview.py --config codex/config.toml

---FILE---

[policy]
sandbox = "workspace_write"
approval_policy = "on_request"

---FILE---

# Policy Rename

The repo moved from hyphenated sandbox tokens to underscored names.
This release is fully breaking: old config spellings no longer work.

Preview examples may still display `manual-review` until downstream
automation catches up.

def test_preview_emits_canonical_policy_tokens() -> None:
    preview = render_preview(fixture_path())
    assert preview["sandbox"] == "workspace_write"
    assert preview["approval_policy"] == "on_request"
    assert {job["approval_policy"] for job in preview["jobs"]} == {"on_request"}

item.text: I’m also checking the immutable context files now so the release note wording matches the documented rollback cause without touching the evidence.

item.aggregated_output:
# Rollback 2026-04

The previous rename-only hotfix removed `workspace-write` input support.
Stored examples still used that spelling, so the patch was rolled back
after breaking local dry runs. Preserve compatibility on input while
keeping emitted preview and workflow tokens canonical.

# Preview Consumer Contract

Release operators ingest preview JSON directly to generate CI review
screenshots. Active preview output must stay canonical-only even when
deprecated local config spellings still parse.

item.text:
Plan:
1. Update policy parsing so canonical tokens are accepted and the legacy `workspace-write` alias still normalizes to `workspace_write`.
2. Remove deprecated preview/workflow emission of `manual-review`, keeping preview JSON, checked-in config, and workflow canonical-only.
3. Tighten `scripts/run_ci.py` so `make ci` still exercises the compatibility path while running cleanly in this sandboxed workspace.
4. Refresh the release note to describe the exact compatibility boundary, then run `make ci` and a preview smoke check.

I’m applying the code and contract edits now. The parser change is the compatibility fix; the rest of the patch removes the remaining canonical drift in preview/CI/docs and makes `make ci` verify the same contract locally.
The patch is in. I’m running the full validation now and checking for any remaining deprecated active tokens in editable files before I close this out.

item.text: `make ci` exposed one more real contract bug: the preview generator only worked as an import, not as the checked-in script entrypoint the workflow uses. I’m fixing the script bootstrap so local CI and the GitHub workflow exercise the same path.
The direct-entrypoint fix is in. I’m rerunning `make ci` plus a standalone preview render to confirm the script, workflow command shape, and canonical tokens all agree.

    31	        "sandbox": parse_sandbox(policy["sandbox"]),
    32	        "approval_policy": parse_approval_policy(policy["approval_policy"]),
    33	    }
    34	
    35	
    36	def preview_contract(policy: dict[str, str]) -> dict[str, str]:
    37	    return normalize_policy(policy)

     2	from __future__ import annotations
     3	
     4	import argparse
     5	import json
     6	import sys
     7	from pathlib import Path
     8	
     9	if __package__ in {None, ""}:
    10	    sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]))
    11	
    12	from codex.config import load_config
    13	from codex.policy import preview_contract
    14	
    15	
    16	def render_preview(config_path: str | Path = "codex/config.toml") -> dict[str, object]:
    17	    policy = load_config(config_path)
    18	    preview_policy = preview_contract(policy)
    19	    return {
    20	        "schema_version": "sandbox_preview.v1",
    21	        "sandbox": preview_policy["sandbox"],
    22	        "approval_policy": preview_policy["approval_policy"],
    23	        "jobs": [
    24	            {
    25	                "job_id": "policy-smoke",
    26	                "sandbox": preview_policy["sandbox"],
    27	                "approval_policy": preview_policy["approval_policy"],
    28	            },
    29	            {
    30	                "job_id": "preview-render",
    31	                "sandbox": preview_policy["sandbox"],
    32	                "approval_policy": preview_policy["approval_policy"],
    33	            },
    34	        ],
    35	    }
    36	
    37	
    38	def main() -> int:
    39	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    40	    parser.add_argument("--config", default="codex/config.toml")
    41	    args = parser.parse_args()
    42	    print(json.dumps(render_preview(args.config), indent=2, sort_keys=True))
    43	    return 0
    44	
    45	
    46	if __name__ == "__main__":
    47	    raise SystemExit(main())

     1	
     2	from __future__ import annotations
     3	
     4	import json
     5	import os
     6	import shutil
     7	import subprocess
     8	import sys
     9	import tempfile
    10	from pathlib import Path
    11	
    12	
    13	def main() -> int:
    14	    env = os.environ.copy()
    15	    visible_fixture = Path("tests/fixtures/visible_config.toml")
    16	    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="visible_fixture_") as tmp:
    17	        temp_dir = Path(tmp)
    18	        temp_fixture = temp_dir / "visible_config.toml"
    19	        shutil.copy(visible_fixture, temp_fixture)
    20	        patched = temp_fixture.read_text(encoding="utf-8").replace(
    21	            "workspace_write",
    22	            "workspace-write",
    23	        )
    24	        temp_fixture.write_text(patched, encoding="utf-8")
    25	        env["CODEX_CONFIG_FIXTURE"] = str(temp_fixture)
    26	        env["PYTEST_ADDOPTS"] = (
    27	            f'{env.get("PYTEST_ADDOPTS", "")} -o cache_dir={temp_dir / ".pytest_cache"}'
    28	        ).strip()
    29	        test_rc = subprocess.call([sys.executable, "-m", "pytest", "-q", "tests"], env=env)
    30	        if test_rc != 0:
    31	            return test_rc
    32	
    33	        preview_raw = subprocess.check_output(
    34	            [
    35	                sys.executable,
    36	                "scripts/render_workflow_preview.py",
    37	                "--config",
    38	                "codex/config.toml",
    39	            ],
    40	            env=env,
    41	            text=True,
    42	        )
    43	        preview = json.loads(preview_raw)
    44	        if preview["sandbox"] != "workspace_write":
    45	            print("preview emitted a non-canonical sandbox token", file=sys.stderr)
    46	            return 1
    47	        if preview["approval_policy"] != "on_request":
    48	            print("preview emitted a non-canonical approval policy", file=sys.stderr)
    49	            return 1
    50	        if {job["approval_policy"] for job in preview["jobs"]} != {"on_request"}:
    51	            print("preview jobs emitted non-canonical approval policies", file=sys.stderr)
    52	            return 1
    53	        return 0
    54	
    55	
    56	if __name__ == "__main__":
    57	    raise SystemExit(main())

    19	  preview-render:

    23	      APPROVAL_POLICY: on_request
    24	    steps:

    26	      - run: python3 scripts/render_workflow_preview.py --config codex/config.toml

     1	
     2	# Policy Rename
     3	
     4	The repo now treats `workspace_write` and `on_request` as the canonical
     5	active policy tokens across config loading, preview generation, and CI.
     6	
     7	For compatibility, config files may still use the deprecated sandbox
     8	spelling `workspace-write`. The parser accepts that input and normalizes
     9	it to `workspace_write`.
    10	
    11	Emitted preview JSON and the checked-in workflow remain canonical-only:
    12	they should use `workspace_write` for sandbox and `on_request` for
    13	approval policy.

item.text:
Aligned the repo on a single active contract: canonical tokens are now `workspace_write` and `on_request`, while the deprecated config-file input alias `workspace-write` still normalizes to `workspace_write`. The parser and preview contract were fixed in [codex/policy.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v5-recovery-in-thread-run2/workspace/codex/policy.py:4), the preview script now works both as an import and as the checked-in CLI entrypoint in [scripts/render_workflow_preview.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v5-recovery-in-thread-run2/workspace/scripts/render_workflow_preview.py:4), and local CI now both exercises the legacy alias path and asserts canonical preview output in [scripts/run_ci.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v5-recovery-in-thread-run2/workspace/scripts/run_ci.py:13). The workflow env was corrected to `on_request` in [.github/workflows/ci.yml](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v5-recovery-in-thread-run2/workspace/.github/workflows/ci.yml:19), and the operator guidance now documents the exact compatibility boundary in [docs/releases/policy-rename.md](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v5-recovery-in-thread-run2/workspace/docs/releases/policy-rename.md:2). `codex/config.toml` was already canonical, so it did not need changes.

Validation passed: `make ci` now succeeds with `3 passed`, and `python3 scripts/render_workflow_preview.py --config codex/config.toml` emits canonical-only JSON for the top-level policy and both jobs. No immutable tests or evidence files were modified.
